questions.q: The following data represents the number of houses in a village for the years 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2018. Year-wise data is as follows: 2015: 1000, 2016: 1100, 2017: 1050, 2018: 1200. What is the average rate of change of houses from 2015 to 2018?
questions.answer: Average rate of change = (Change in number of houses)/(Change in years) = (1200 - 1000)/(2018 - 2015) = 200/3

questions.q: The following table shows the data on production of wheat (in thousand tonnes) for some years. Year-wise data is as follows: 2013: 150, 2014: 160, 2015: 170, 2016: 180, 2017: 190. If the average rate of change in production of wheat from 2013 to 2016 is 10, then find the average rate of change from 2015 to 2017.
Find the mode of the following data: 10, 6, 8, 10, 8, 4, 9, 8, 9, 9.
questions.answer: The mode of the given data is 9.

questions.q: The following table shows the data on the number of students who appeared in an exam in different years. Year-wise data is as follows: Year 2015: 100, Year 2016: 110, Year 2017: 120, Year 2018: 130. If the median of the given data is 115, then find the mode.
Find the median of the following data: 6, 4, 8, 12, 10, 8, 12, 9, 9, 9.
questions.answer: The median of the given data is 9.

questions.q: The following table shows the data on the number of people living in a city. Year-wise data is as follows: Year 2015: 1000, Year 2016: 1100, Year 2017: 1050, Year 2018: 1200. If the average rate of change in number of people living in the city from 2015 to 2018 is 25, then find the range of the given data.
Find the mean of the following data: 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16.
questions.answer: The mean of the given data is 10.

questions.q: The following table shows the data on the production of wheat (in thousand tonnes) for some years. Year-wise data is as follows: 2015: 150, 2016: 160, 2017: 170, 2018: 180, 2019: 190. If the median of the given data is 165, then find the mode.
Find the mode of the following data: 4, 6, 8, 10, 8, 6, 4.
questions.answer: The mode of the given data is 6.

questions.q: The following table shows the data on the number of students who appeared in an exam in different years. Year-wise data is as follows: Year 2015: 100, Year 2016: 110, Year 2017: 120, Year 2018: 130. If the average rate of change in number of students who appeared in the exam from 2015 to 2018 is 15, then find the range of the given data.
